summary: Frontend-only regression after navigation consolidation (Gaming hub with 2 tabs, MyPc hub with 2 tabs, renamed sidebar entries, grouped sections, onboarding box). All requested flows PASS.

test_results.onboarding_box_visible: PASS (onboarding-box, step-connect, step-optimize, step-track all visible with correct hrefs /app/desktop, /app/advisor, /app/tracker)
test_results.sidebar_sections: PASS (Ottimizza il PC, Gaming, Acquisti sections all rendered)
test_results.sidebar_nav_all_items: PASS (all 11 entries: dashboard, pc, advisor, commands, bios, desktop, gaming, builds, upgrade, tracker, admin navigate and render without errors/blank)
test_results.gaming_tabs: PASS (gaming-tab-games and gaming-tab-profiles switch content correctly)
test_results.mypc_tabs: PASS (mypc-tab-overview and mypc-tab-live switch content; live-page and alert-settings render)
test_results.deep_link_/app/live: PASS (opens MyPc hub with Live tab active)
test_results.deep_link_/app/profiles: PASS (opens Gaming with Profiles tab active)
test_results.deep_link_/app/games: PASS (opens Gaming with Games tab active)
test_results.prematch_toggle_and_save: PASS (config toggle reveals groups; PUT /api/prematch save completes without error)
test_results.fps_estimate_via_chip: PASS (game-chip-0 -> Counter-Strike 2 -> fps-result rendered with Basso/Medio/Alto/Ultra bars, preset Medio)

critical_code_review_comments: Layout.jsx page-title lookup uses NAV.find with location.pathname.startsWith(n.to). Since Dashboard '/app' is not marked end in the find, and '/app/pc' starts with '/app', it works because Dashboard has end:true and returns match only for exact '/app'. OK.
Gaming/MyPcHub tab state is local; navigating between /app/gaming and /app/profiles remounts and picks initialTab correctly (verified). No key issue.
Minor: sidebar 'Gaming' section only contains one item — consider merging into parent or dropping the section label for consistency; low priority.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Navigation consolidation verified end-to-end. All data-testid selectors specified in the review request exist and function. FPS estimate via Claude took ~5-10s in this run.
